query: Compare Medicaid versus provider manual guidance for dual eligible pharmacy claims.
answer: {"headline_summary": "The Provider Manual, effective October 1, 2025, offers more recent and specific guidance on dual eligible pharmacy claims compared to the latest relevant Policy Update from December 1, 2024, and should receive more weight by default.", "policy_definition": "The most recent policy update (December 1, 2024) generally advises practitioners to limit NYS Medicaid claim amounts to the actual invoice cost of the drug dosage administered and to refer to eMedNY provider manual web pages for additional program policy and billing guidance [New York State Medicaid Update December 2024 Volume 40 Number 13:4 — Dec 01, 2024]. Older policy guidance (August 1, 2021) specified that pharmacies could only bill for Medicaid non-dual eligible members for immunization services, with dual eligible members continuing to access immunization services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — Aug 01, 2021]. Other policy updates (April 1, 2023, and January 1, 2024) direct providers to specific eMedNY Provider Manuals for pharmacy claims guidance [New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — Apr 01, 2023; New York State Medicaid Update January 2024 Volume 40 Number 1:7, 8 — Jan 01, 2024].", "provider_manual_definition": "For dual eligible pharmacy claims, the total Medicare/Medicaid payment to the pharmacy provider will not exceed the amount that the pharmacy provider would have received for a Medicaid-only patient. If the Medicare payment is greater than the Medicaid fee, no additional payment will be made, and the Medicare and Medicaid payment (if any) must be accepted as payment in full [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — Oct 01, 2025]. Per state regulation, a pharmacy provider of a Medicare Part B benefit cannot seek to recover any Medicare Part B deductible or coinsurance amounts from Medicaid dual eligible members [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18 — Oct 01, 2025]. The NYS Medicaid program requires Medicare enrollment for pharmacies enrolling in Medicaid to ensure dual eligible members receive drugs and supplies from Medicaid providers enrolled in accordance with federal and State laws and regulations [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21 — Oct 01, 2025]. All Medicaid enrolled pharmacy providers are required by federal law to submit claims and accept assignments for Medicare covered services provided to Medicaid dual eligible members, regardless of CMS-460 enrollment status [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — Oct 01, 2025]. Pharmacies missing one or more Medicare enrollments must determine if their business situation or business model requires CMS-855B or CMS-855S Medicare enrollment or both before applying for NY Medicaid Pharmacy enrollment [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:21, 22 — Oct 01, 2025].", "similarities": ["Both policy updates and provider manual guidance indicate that detailed instructions for pharmacy claims, including those potentially for dual eligible members, are primarily found within the eMedNY provider manuals [New York State Medicaid Update December 2024 Volume 40 Number 13:4 — Dec 01, 2024; New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8:9 — Apr 01, 2023; New York State Medicaid Update January 2024 Volume 40 Number 1:7, 8 — Jan 01, 2024]."], "differences": ["The provider manual provides specific, detailed guidance on payment limitations, Medicare enrollment requirements, and claim submission obligations for dual eligible pharmacy claims [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:18, 21, 22 — Oct 01, 2025], whereas the most recent policy updates primarily refer providers to the manual for such details [New York State Medicaid Update December 2024 Volume 40 Number 13:4 — Dec 01, 2024].", "An older policy update (August 2021) explicitly stated that pharmacies could only bill for Medicaid non-dual eligible members for immunization services, with dual eligible members accessing these services through Medicare [New York State Medicaid Update August 2021 Volume 37 Number 10:9 — Aug 01, 2021], a specific detail regarding immunization services not reiterated in the provided provider manual excerpts."], "caveats": null}
